motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 4°F at first light, rising no higher than 9°F by midday. Sky the white of old paper, close and still. No wind. The lake sealed entirely, shore to shore, one unbroken pane. Smoke from every chimney going straight up.

undercurrent.note: CRESTING and beginning to turn. Both letters to T. Aldercott have been collected. The box is at thirteen. The channel has delivered what it came to deliver — two arrivals, one who indexes and one who carries a typewriter — and the hum at dusk was shorter than it has been, which is the first sign of the arc pulling back. The lake has said what it came to say. The town is now in the position of having received the message and not yet knowing what to do with having received it, which is the precise emotional register of the season's peak. The undercurrent holds high because resolution has not yet begun — the box is still open, the rowboat has not moved, Agnes has not gone inside for good — but the note of urgency is dropping. The level edges down because the second arrival has arrived: the channel has been answered. What comes next is not escalation but the long, quiet, warm work of accommodation.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
